Who's teaching our kids?

Chris Cantergiani WSB-TV Channel 2/Atlanta May 2000
Government & PoliticsBusiness & EconomyInvestigative Methods

This investigation revealed critical flaws in Georgia's teacher certification process. It was found that nearly 3,000 school employees had criminal records, including felony convictions. The system he...

This investigation revealed critical flaws in Georgia's teacher certification process. It was found that nearly 3,000 school employees had criminal records, including felony convictions. The system heavily relied on applicants to self-disclose past convictions. Using databases sourced from state departments and a comprehensive analysis over a 26-hour stretch, the report exposed these oversights. Schools planned to improve monitoring and close loopholes in light of the findings, which shocked parents and state officials.
